positions_left_in_code.reason: The 20×20 collision box for trees is a fixed gameplay constant shared by all tree instances. It is not a visual coordinate and is not scene-specific layout data. Extracting it would conflate collision physics constants with layout positions. Left in code as a named constant (20×20) consistent with how the original was written.

notes: All computed positions resolved against canvas size (width=900, height=700): grass_field, path_center_vertical, path_bottom, fence_top, fence_bottom_left, fence_bottom_right, fence_left, fence_right, gate_sign, scene_title, exit_south all carry _note fields documenting the original formula.
fence_posts loop parameters (x_start, x_step, gap_min_x, gap_max_x, y_top, y_bottom, w, h) extracted as a single object. Loop body in code now iterates using this.layout.fence_posts fields.
Dog wander tweens remain dynamic (random offsets applied at runtime). Dog spawn positions (dog_spots) extracted to JSON as static anchors; tween targets reference dog.x/dog.y from the layout array.

Original dogPositions array had unused 'speed' properties on each dog object — speed was never read anywhere in the tween or loop. These were not extracted (speed is not a layout concern). The dogEmojis array in the refactored code replaces the per-object emoji property to preserve the same rendering.
Tree collision box (20×20) left in code — see positions_left_in_code.
Self-audit: grep for literal coords in this.add.* calls returned zero results. All 26 JSON keys confirmed referenced via this.layout.<name> in the scene file.
JSON is 121 lines — well under 200-line limit.
static layoutEditorConfig added immediately after class declaration opening brace with verbatim layoutAssetKey and layoutPath per dispatch parameters.
loadLayout.js and CLAUDE.md already existed from prior dispatches — not touched (idempotent).
